title: Valid Parentheses
approach: Use a stack. Push each opening bracket. On a closing bracket, pop and verify it matches; otherwise return False. String is valid iff the stack is empty at the end.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
approach: Two-pointer merge into a dummy-headed list. Advance whichever pointer holds the smaller value, attach the remainder at the end.

title: Maximum Subarray
approach: Kadane's algorithm. Track the best sum ending at index i as max(nums[i], best_prev + nums[i]); the answer is the max of these running values. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Climbing Stairs
approach: Classic Fibonacci: f(n) = f(n-1) + f(n-2), with f(1)=1, f(2)=2. Iterate with two rolling variables. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
approach: Single pass. Track the minimum price seen so far and the best profit obtainable if selling today (price - min_so_far).

title: Contains Duplicate
approach: Compare set size to list size. If any element repeats, the set is smaller.
code:
def containsDuplicate(nums: list[int]) -> bool:
    return len(set(nums)) != len(nums)

title: Product of Array Except Self
approach: Two passes with a single output array. First pass fills answer[i] with the prefix product of everything to the left. Second pass multiplies in the suffix product on the way back, using a running variable. O(n) time, O(1) extra space (excluding output).

title: Single Number
approach: XOR every element together. Duplicate pairs cancel to 0; the lone element remains. O(n) time, O(1) space.

title: Move Zeroes
approach: Two-pointer in-place partition. Walk with i; whenever nums[i] is non-zero, swap it into position write and increment write. Zeros end up at the tail.

title: Reverse Linked List
approach: Iterative pointer reversal. Track prev; for each node, save next, redirect node.next to prev, advance both pointers. Return prev at the end.
